reasoning: The document has 4 major programmatic defects regarding structural accessibility: retained headers/footers, a list broken by a page break, incorrect list semantics (Roman numerals rendered as alphabetical), and truncated image alt text. It also has one minor LLM fidelity defect for lost visual emphasis. Based on the count-based threshold of 4 major defects, it must be routed to HUMAN_REVIEW.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (6): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Reso; Page 2 of 6; Page 3 of 6
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=6, pages_affected=6/6. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (1 instance(s)): p4→p5: list item ends “…, permeable reactive barriers and volume”, continues as paragraph
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=1. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 1 ordered list(s) rendered as alphabetical (A, B, C) but contain roman numeral markers (I, II, III) — e.g. "II. March 22, 2016 Public Meeting and Oral Comments Summary"
concerns.analysis: roman_letter_confusion=1. The renderer misclassified roman numeral lists as alphabetical starting at letter 'I'. Item text retains original roman numerals alongside incorrect letter markers, breaking list semantics.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 1 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (1 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...ters "NC", with the 'N' in shades of green and the"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=1 (truncated=1, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

The source document heavily uses underline formatting for emphasis on the bulleted sub-headings on pages 3, 4, and 5 (e.g., 'Beneficial Reuse', 'Dam Safety', 'Excavation'). This underline styling is entirely absent in the output, which simply outputs the plain text (and occasionally adds markdown bold). Underline represents deliberate visual emphasis in the source document that is lost.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Underlined text: "Beneficial Reuse", "Dam Safety", "Excavation" (and all other list sub-headings)
concerns.output_quote: "Beneficial Reuse – Comments suggested...", "Dam Safety – Comments were made..."

action_items: Remove retained header/footer text from the HTML.
Merge the broken list item across the page break.
Correct the list marker semantics to reflect Roman numerals instead of alphabetical markers.
Fix the truncated image alt text.
Restore visual emphasis (underline or strong) for sub-headings if semantically meaningful.
